
```{r}
install.packages("factoextra", dependencies = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# ================================
# 0) LIBRAIRIES
# ================================
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
library(randomForest)
library(caret)
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 1) IMPORTATION DES DONNEES
# ================================
df <- read.csv(file.choose(), sep = ";")

head(df)
str(df)
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 2) NETTOYAGE ET PREPARATION
# ================================

# Supprimer id s'il existe
if ("id" %in% names(df)) {
  df$id <- NULL
}

# Création du BMI
df$BMI <- df$weight / (df$height / 100)^2

# Conversion de l'âge en années
df$age_years <- df$age / 365

# ===== NEW FEATURES =====
df$pulse_pressure <- df$ap_hi - df$ap_lo
df$mean_arterial_pressure <- (df$ap_hi + 2 * df$ap_lo) / 3

# Conversion des variables catégorielles en facteurs
factor_vars <- c("cardio", "gender", "cholesterol", "gluc", "smoke", "alco", "active")

for (v in factor_vars) {
  if (v %in% names(df)) {
    df[[v]] <- as.factor(df[[v]])
  }
}
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 3) SUPPRESSION DES VALEURS ABERRANTES
# ================================
# On filtre les valeurs physiologiquement incohérentes
df <- subset(df, ap_hi > 0 & ap_hi < 300)
df <- subset(df, ap_lo > 0 & ap_lo < 200)
df <- subset(df, ap_hi > ap_lo)
df <- subset(df, BMI > 10 & BMI < 80)
df <- subset(df, height > 100 & height < 250)
df <- subset(df, weight > 20 & weight < 300)

# Vérification après nettoyage
dim(df)
str(df)
```
```{r}
table(df$active)
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 4) EXPLORATION DES DONNEES
# ================================
dim(df)
colSums(is.na(df))
summary(df)

summary(df$age_years)
summary(df$BMI)
summary(df$ap_hi)
summary(df$ap_lo)

aggregate(age_years ~ cardio, data = df, mean)
aggregate(BMI ~ cardio, data = df, mean)

# Proportion malades / non malades
prop.table(table(df$cardio))
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 5) VISUALISATIONS
# ================================

# Histogramme de l'âge
ggplot(df, aes(x = age_years)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution de l'âge", x = "Âge (années)", y = "Effectif")

# Boxplot BMI selon cardio
ggplot(df, aes(x = cardio, y = BMI)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue") +
  labs(title = "BMI selon la présence de maladie", x = "Cardio", y = "BMI")

# Histogramme pression systolique
ggplot(df, aes(x = ap_hi)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "orange", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution de la pression systolique", x = "ap_hi", y = "Effectif")

# Histogramme pression diastolique
ggplot(df, aes(x = ap_lo)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "tomato", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution de la pression diastolique", x = "ap_lo", y = "Effectif")
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 6) MATRICE DE CORRELATION
# ================================
numeric_vars <- df[, c(
  "age_years",
  "BMI",
  "ap_hi",
  "ap_lo",
  "pulse_pressure",
  "mean_arterial_pressure"
)]

corrplot(cor(numeric_vars), method = "color", addCoef.col = "black")
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 7) ACP (PCA)
# ================================
# On garde seulement les variables quantitatives
pca_data <- df[, c("age_years", "BMI", "ap_hi", "ap_lo")]

res.pca <- PCA(pca_data, scale.unit = TRUE, graph = FALSE)

# Visualisation des individus (version légère)
fviz_pca_ind(
  res.pca,
  habillage = df$cardio,
  label = "none",
  title = "ACP des individus selon cardio"
)

# Visualisation des variables
fviz_pca_var(
  res.pca,
  repel = TRUE,
  title = "ACP - Cercle des corrélations"
)
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 8) CLUSTERING K-MEANS
# ================================
data_cluster <- scale(pca_data)

set.seed(123)
kmeans_model <- kmeans(data_cluster, centers = 3, nstart = 25)

df$cluster_kmeans <- as.factor(kmeans_model$cluster)

# Visualisation simple des clusters
plot(
  df$age_years, df$BMI,
  col = df$cluster_kmeans,
  pch = 19,
  xlab = "Age (années)",
  ylab = "BMI",
  main = "Clusters K-means"
)

# Tableau cluster / cardio
table(df$cluster_kmeans, df$cardio)
```
```{r}
set.seed(123)
sample_idx <- sample(1:nrow(data_cluster), 5000)
data_sample <- data_cluster[sample_idx, ]

fviz_nbclust(data_sample, kmeans, method = "wss")
```
```{r}
aggregate(cbind(age_years, BMI, ap_hi, ap_lo) ~ cluster_kmeans, data = df, mean)
```
```{r}
table(df$cluster_kmeans, df$cardio)
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 9) CLUSTERING HIERARCHIQUE SUR ECHANTILLON
# ================================
# HCPC sur tout le dataset est trop lourd en mémoire
set.seed(123)
idx_hc <- sample(1:nrow(df), 2000)

pca_data_sample <- pca_data[idx_hc, ]
cardio_sample <- df$cardio[idx_hc]

res.pca.sample <- PCA(pca_data_sample, scale.unit = TRUE, graph = FALSE)

hcpc_res <- HCPC(res.pca.sample, graph = FALSE)

# Visualisation clustering hiérarchique
fviz_cluster(
  hcpc_res,
  geom = "point",
  main = "Clustering hiérarchique après ACP (échantillon)"
)

# Tableau cluster hiérarchique / cardio
table(hcpc_res$data.clust$clust, cardio_sample)
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 10) TRAIN / TEST SPLIT
# ================================
set.seed(123)
index <- sample(1:nrow(df), 0.7 * nrow(df))

train <- df[index, ]
test  <- df[-index, ]
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 11) REGRESSION LOGISTIQUE - MODELE INITIAL
# ================================
model_log <- glm(
  cardio ~ age_years + BMI + ap_hi + ap_lo +
  gender + cholesterol + gluc + smoke + alco + active,
  data = train,
  family = binomial
)


summary(model_log)
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 12) SELECTION DE VARIABLES BACKWARD
# ================================
model_step <- step(model_log, direction = "backward", trace = TRUE)

summary(model_step)
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 13) PREDICTIONS MODELE LOGISTIQUE FINAL
# ================================
pred_prob_log <- predict(model_step, newdata = test, type = "response")
pred_log <- ifelse(pred_prob_log > 0.5, 1, 0)
# TEST AUTRE SEUIL
pred_log_04 <- ifelse(pred_prob_log > 0.4, 1, 0)
pred_log_04 <- factor(pred_log_04, levels = levels(test$cardio))

confusionMatrix(pred_log_04, test$cardio, positive = "1")
pred_log <- as.factor(pred_log)

# Remettre les mêmes niveaux que test$cardio
pred_log <- factor(pred_log, levels = levels(test$cardio))

# Matrice de confusion
cm_log <- confusionMatrix(pred_log, test$cardio, positive = "1")
cm_log

# Precision, Recall, F1-score
precision_log <- cm_log$byClass["Precision"]
recall_log <- cm_log$byClass["Recall"]
f1_log <- 2 * (precision_log * recall_log) / (precision_log + recall_log)

precision_log
recall_log
f1_log
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 14) RANDOM FOREST
# ================================
set.seed(123)
control <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5)

grid_rf <- expand.grid(mtry = c(3,4,5,6))

rf_tuned <- train(
cardio ~ age_years + BMI + ap_hi + ap_lo + pulse_pressure + mean_arterial_pressure +
gender + cholesterol + gluc + smoke + alco + active,
data = train,
method = "rf",
trControl = control,
tuneGrid = grid_rf,
ntree = 500
)

rf_tuned

print(rf_tuned)

pred_rf <- predict(rf_tuned, newdata = test)
cm_rf <- confusionMatrix(pred_rf, test$cardio, positive="1")



# Precision, Recall, F1-score
precision_rf <- cm_rf$byClass["Precision"]
recall_rf <- cm_rf$byClass["Recall"]
f1_rf <- 2 * (precision_rf * recall_rf) / (precision_rf + recall_rf)

precision_rf
recall_rf
f1_rf
```
```{r}
importance <- varImp(rf_tuned)
plot(importance)
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 15) COMPARAISON DES MODELES
# ================================
results <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Logistic Stepwise", "Random Forest"),
  Accuracy = c(as.numeric(cm_log$overall["Accuracy"]), as.numeric(cm_rf$overall["Accuracy"])),
  Precision = c(as.numeric(precision_log), as.numeric(precision_rf)),
  Recall = c(as.numeric(recall_log), as.numeric(recall_rf)),
  F1_Score = c(as.numeric(f1_log), as.numeric(f1_rf))
)

print(results)
```


```{r}
# ================================
# 16) BONUS : AJOUT DU CLUSTER K-MEANS AU MODELE LOGISTIQUE
# ================================
train$cluster_kmeans <- as.factor(train$cluster_kmeans)
test$cluster_kmeans  <- as.factor(test$cluster_kmeans)

model_log_cluster <- glm(
  cardio ~ age_years + BMI + ap_hi + ap_lo + gender + cholesterol + gluc + smoke + alco + active + cluster_kmeans,
  data = train,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model_log_cluster)

pred_prob_cluster <- predict(model_log_cluster, newdata = test, type = "response")
pred_cluster <- ifelse(pred_prob_cluster > 0.5, 1, 0)
pred_cluster <- as.factor(pred_cluster)
pred_cluster <- factor(pred_cluster, levels = levels(test$cardio))

cm_cluster <- confusionMatrix(pred_cluster, test$cardio, positive = "1")
cm_cluster

precision_cluster <- cm_cluster$byClass["Precision"]
recall_cluster <- cm_cluster$byClass["Recall"]
f1_cluster <- 2 * (precision_cluster * recall_cluster) / (precision_cluster + recall_cluster)

precision_cluster
recall_cluster
f1_cluster
```
